abseqr 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Analysing Nanobody CDR3 Sequences with abseqr

Extract CDR loops from a Chothia-renumbered PDB tibble

Source: R/chothia_pdb.R
extract_cdr_chothia.Rd

Uses standard Chothia residue number ranges to extract CDR1, CDR2, and CDR3 residues from the output of read_chothia_pdb(). Insertion codes (e.g. residues 100A–100K in the H-CDR3 loop) are automatically included within the CDR3 range.

extract_cdr_chothia(pdb_atoms, chain_types = c("H", "L"))

Arguments

pdb_atoms

A tibble as returned by read_chothia_pdb().

chain_types

Character vector of chain types to extract CDRs for. Default c("H", "L"). Use "H" for heavy chain only or "L" for light chain only.

Value

A tibble with all columns from pdb_atoms, plus:

cdr

Character. CDR label ("CDR1", "CDR2", "CDR3"), or NA if the residue is not in a CDR.

Only rows that fall within a CDR are returned.

Examples

pdb_file <- system.file("extdata", "1f2x.pdb", package = "abseqr")
atoms <- read_chothia_pdb(pdb_file)
cdrs  <- extract_cdr_chothia(atoms)
cdrs
#> # A tibble: 60 × 14
#>    record atom_serial atom_name res_name chain_id res_seq res_num ins_code
#>    <chr>        <int> <chr>     <chr>    <chr>    <chr>     <int> <chr>   
#>  1 ATOM           161 CA        GLY      K        26           26 ""      
#>  2 ATOM           165 CA        TYR      K        27           27 ""      
#>  3 ATOM           177 CA        THR      K        28           28 ""      
#>  4 ATOM           184 CA        VAL      K        29           29 ""      
#>  5 ATOM           191 CA        SER      K        30           30 ""      
#>  6 ATOM           197 CA        THR      K        31           31 ""      
#>  7 ATOM           204 CA        TYR      K        32           32 ""      
#>  8 ATOM           361 CA        LEU      K        53           53 ""      
#>  9 ATOM           369 CA        GLY      K        54           54 ""      
#> 10 ATOM           373 CA        GLY      K        55           55 ""      
#> # ℹ 50 more rows
#> # ℹ 6 more variables: x <dbl>, y <dbl>, z <dbl>, aa1 <chr>, chain_type <chr>,
#> #   cdr <chr>
